name: Chinese Hot and Sour Soup
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tsp Rice wine or dry sherry
6 1/2 cups Chicken broth or white stock
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak wood ears and lily buds in 3 cups hot water for 15 minutes to soften.
2. Remove stems from softened wood ears. Shred the wood ears with a cleaver.
3. Slice pork into thin strips. Use a cleaver to chop the strips into shreds.
4. Combine marinade ingredients in a small bowl.
5. Add pork shreds; mix well. Let stand for 15 minutes.
6. In a medium saucepan, combine chicken broth and salt. Bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to medium.
7. Add mushrooms, water chestnuts, bamboo shoots, wood ears, and lily buds to the broth. Simmer for 3 to 4 minutes.
8. Add pork shreds with marinade and bean curd to the soup. Bring to a boil.
9. Add Worcestershire sauce and vinegar if desired.
10. Dissolve cornstarch in 5 tablespoons water to make a paste.
11. Slowly stir the paste into the soup. Cook over medium heat until the soup thickens slightly.
12. Stir egg into the soup.
13. Add black pepper, white pepper, and sesame oil.
